issues.title: Incorrect table names
issues.problem: The query uses incorrect table names 'users' and 'orders' instead of 'sigma_analytics.dim_customer' and 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Missing join condition
issues.problem: The query uses implicit join syntax which is less readable and can lead to errors.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN with a subquery to calculate the average amount per user.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of the query and each clause.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, t.transaction_id AS order_id
FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c
JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.amount > (
    SELECT AVG(amount)
    FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions
    WHERE customer_id = c.customer_id
);
review.summary: The query had several critical correctness issues due to incorrect table names and missing join conditions. It also contained a correlated subquery that could be optimized and lacked comments for readability.
